data.title: Barrick seeks help from World Bank court on government takeover of Mali mine
data.description:
Barrick Mining (NYSE:B) has asked the World Bank's arbitration tribunal to intervene in legal proceedings in Mali, Reuters reported late Thursday, as the company faces the possibility of the Loulo-Gounkoto gold mine falling under the control of the Mali government.

Barrick's (NYSE:B) request for "provisional measures" from the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes comes as a local court in Mali is expected to rule on June 2 on the government's request to put the company's mine under a provisional administration; if the Mali court ruling goes in favor of the government, an individual or an organization would be assigned to take over the mine and reopen it.

An official familiar with the Mali government's thinking told Reuters that there are no grounds for international arbitration, and it is a domestic tax issue; even if the World Bank tribunal rules in favor of Barrick's (B) request for a provisional measure, the government could ignore it, although Mali would risk further scaring off Western investors.

Barrick's (B) Loulo-Gounkoto mine has been shut since January after Mali seized three tons of gold over non-payment of taxes, an allegation the company has denied.

data.title: CollPlant Biotechnologies shares slide after announces capital raise $3.6M through registered direct offering
Digital identity: EU reveals upcoming age-verification app
data.description:
The "EU Digital Identity Wallet" is set to roll out next year, which will offer the ability for citizens to prove who they are online without giving over their information to external websites. The effort is being aimed at an increasingly digitized world, and will impact everything from travel to ordering prescriptions from pharmacies. The wallet would also give the ability to store verified electronic documents, like tax forms, notarized filings, and personal work certifications, as well as driving licenses and payment cards.

Backdrop: The latest project followed the adoption of the eIDAS regulation in 2014. In 2021, the European Commission proposed a revision called the European Digital Identity Framework, which was updated last year to lay down the rules for the core functionalities and certification of eID Wallets. The latter will be recognized throughout Europe, assisting with domestic and cross-border transactions without the need to remember countless passwords. 

Ahead of the launch of the "EU Digital Identity Wallet," the bloc just revealed that a new white label age-verification app will be out by the summer. The latter could allow for stricter policing and protections for minors online, helping to shield them from accessing harmful content and websites. Services like Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Meta (NASDAQ:META) are already being probed by the EU under the Digital Markets Act, and the latest push toward digital identification may give fresh impetus to those investigations.

Privacy concerns? It may only be a matter of time before the "EU Digital Identity Wallet" is required by the government or is demanded by the private sector as a strong form of identification. Untold amounts of personal information, transactions, and other data - all stored under one framework - could become a hacker's paradise despite selective disclosure capabilities. There are also worries about mass surveillance and the potential for rating systems or scores, though others say that ship has sailed a long time ago for any modern digital economy.

data.title: Ralph Lauren attracts a bull rating from Deutsche Bank
data.description:
Deutsche Bank resumed coverage on Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) with a Buy rating in a note titled "Not Your Grandpa's Polo." 

Analyst Krisztina Katai highlighted that few companies within the DB's coverage fit the description of high-quality with good underlying fundamentals, pricing power, white space opportunity, and limited China sourcing, but the firm thinks Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) checks all the boxes.

"We believe the market still underappreciates the transformational changes, distribution and sourcing model, and margin expansion potential, but a positive EPS revision cycle ahead should help move both estimates and valuation higher."

Ralph Lauren's (RL) Q1 results and FQ1 trends were noted to further increase DB's confidence. The luxury apparel maker's sound balance sheet, attractive free cash flow yield, and robust buyback program are seen helping to support double-digit earnings growth.

Deutsche Bank assigned a price target of $343 to Ralph Lauren (RL), implying 25% potential upside including the dividend yield. 

Shares of Ralph Lauren (RL) were up 0.3% in premarket trading to $278.37 vs. the 52-week range of $155.96 to $289.33. Short interest on RL stands at 7.1% of the total float.

data.title: UiPath gains as earnings beat, growth prospects give investors some reasons to cheer
data.description:
UiPath (NYSE:PATH) shares rose ~13% in premarket trading on Friday as investors cheered the company's April quarter numbers and guidance, released post-market in the previous session.

The company projected FY revenue in the range of $1.549 billion to $1.554 billion, compared to the consensus of $1.52 billion. This comes along with a healthy first-quarter print. ARR of $1.693 billion meanwhile increased 12% year-over-year.

UiPath's (NYSE:PATH) low expectations and improving fundamentals set up an attractive risk-reward, with revenue growth likely to accelerate into the mid-teens, Michael Wiggins De Oliveira, a Seeking Alpha investing group leader, said.

"Recent product launches and partnerships with Microsoft (MSFT) and Google Cloud (GOOG) validate UiPath's near-term prospects and potential for renewed momentum," De Oliveira added.

UiPath (PATH) CEO Daniel Dines highlighted the momentum following the launch of the agentic automation platform, describing it as "one of the most important and successful product introductions in our history." 

Dines also referenced a "multiyear, multimillion-dollar expansion" with a Fortune 15 health company and significant partnerships, including a new AI partnership.

When Sheldon McMeans of Barclays asked about macroeconomic uncertainty and FX impact, CFO Ashim Gupta responded, "The macroeconomic environment, it is still variable... we kind of adjusted our linearity as such, which for me is a favorable move from a revenue standpoint." On FX: "It really was pretty minimal... nothing significant or material that we would factor in."

However, Richard Durant of Narweena said that while UiPath has a dominant position in a large market, its financial performance remains uninspiring.

"...UiPath probably needs a positive catalyst to drive the stock significantly higher."

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant ratings, the company has a Hold rating with a score of 3.32 out of 5.

data.title: Canopy Growth in charts: FQ4 Canada cannabis net revenue up 4.6% Y/Y, Storz & Bickel -22.8%
Mainstreet Equity to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Mainstreet Equity (TSX:MEQ:CA) said the Toronto Stock Exchange has accepted its notice of intention to make a normal course issuer bid.
  • The firm may purchase up to 475.4K shares under the bid.

data.title: Galaxy Digital prices upsized offering of common stock at $19 per share
data.description:
Galaxy Digital (OTCPK:BRPHF) priced its offering of class A common stock at $19 per share, and increased the size of the offering to 31.6M shares from 29.0M.

The company is offering 26.4M shares and certain other shareholders are offering 5.20M shares in the public offering.

Galaxy (OTCPK:BRPHF) plans to use net proceeds from the sale to purchase newly issued limited partnership units from its operating subsidiary, Galaxy Digital Holdings LP, which will use those proceeds to finance the expansion of its artificial intelligence and high-performance computing infrastructure at its Helios data center campus.

The offering's underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to 4.74M additional secondary shares at the public offering less the underwriting discount.

The offering is expected to close on June 3, 2025.

data.title: Kits Eyecare looks to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Kits Eyecare (KITS:CA) said the Toronto Stock Exchange has accepted its notice of intention to make a normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the bid, the firm may buy back up to a maximum of 1.6M shares.

data.title: Canopy Growth GAAP EPS of -C$1.43 misses by C$0.88, revenue of C$65.03M misses by C$5.54M
data.description:
• Canopy Growth press release (NASDAQ:CGC): Q4 GAAP EPS of -C$1.43 misses by C$0.88.
  • Revenue of C$65.03M (-10.7% Y/Y) misses by C$5.54M.
  • Additional cost reduction initiatives identified and initiated in Q4 FY2025 are expected to deliver at least C$20 million in annualized savings over the next 12-18 months
